description_long: This object consists of five deciduous trees with snow-covered canopies, arranged in a row. Each tree features a relatively straight, brown trunk supporting a dense, rounded crown of branches and leaves, all heavily dusted with white snow. The snow gives the foliage a frosted, wintery appearance, with some subtle hints of green and brown visible beneath the snow layer. The trunks are slender and taper slightly toward the base, with no visible roots above ground. The trees are of similar height and shape, suggesting they are the same species and age, and are commonly found in urban parks or along city streets during winter. The overall impression is of a small, uniform group of ornamental street or park trees in a snowy environment.
description: A group of five snow-covered deciduous trees with dense, rounded canopies and slender brown trunks, arranged in a row. The foliage is heavily frosted, giving a wintry, ornamental appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing all five trees in a row, highlighting their dense, snow-covered canopies and straight trunks.
description_view_1: Side view showing the narrow profile of the group, with the canopies aligned and the trunks mostly hidden behind each other.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the snow-laden crowns and the arrangement of the trees from the opposite side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the narrow profile and the alignment of the trees' canopies.
